brands.name: The Joint Chiropractic
brands.notes: Retail-model chiropractic clinic franchise (The Joint Corp., NASDAQ: JYNT) with 950+ US clinics and a membership/recurring-revenue model. Ranked the No. 1 chiropractic franchise in Entrepreneur's Franchise 500; franchise sales run through thejointfranchise.com.

riskFlags.title: Short-seller scrutiny of disclosures and clinic re-acquisitions
riskFlags.detail: The Bear Cave investigation reported The Joint had re-acquired roughly 50 struggling franchises (including 8 in April 2021), excluded over half of locations from its average net-profit calculations, and reduced clinic-profitability disclosures; the public company has cycled through five CFOs and three auditors since its 2014 IPO.

riskFlags.title: Consumer complaints and state regulatory inquiries over membership billing
riskFlags.detail: Complaints to the Texas Attorney General described the recurring-billing membership model as continuously charging credit cards monthly; the company has faced regulatory inquiries about its chiropractic care in at least seven states, and a Texas doctor/whistleblower alleged retaliation after reporting issues.

nationwide.source: The Joint Chiropractic franchise site - Available Territories and Growth Markets
nationwide.note: Franchisor states it has 'prime territories available across the country' and is 'ramping up for a nationwide expansion', listing open markets in 30+ states (accessed June 2026).

liquidCapitalMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $250,000 cash requirement (net worth $500,000); Vetted Biz lists a $65,000 minimum cash figure — conflict; Entrepreneur (more recent) used.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD Item 7 range $245,250–$543,000; franchisor site confirms ownership 'begins at $245,250'.

franchiseFee.note: Vetted Biz shows an FDD fee range of $19,950–$39,900; $39,900 is the standard single-unit fee.

royaltyPercent.note: 7% of gross sales; confirmed by Vetted Biz 2025 FDD summary.

adFundPercent.note: Entrepreneur and Vetted Biz list 2%; some aggregators report a 3% marketing contribution — conflict noted.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19-derived (franchisor-published): average 2024 gross sales across 785 franchised clinics open the full calendar year; top quartile averaged $913,466.

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 (2026) unit count; franchisor cites '950+ locations'.

yearFounded.note: Founded 1999; The Joint Corp. introduced the retail healthcare model in 2010.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 3–5 employees required; lower bound shown.

veteranDiscount.note: $6,000 off first-unit franchise fee for veterans.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchisor promotes multi-location ownership and area development (e.g., 25-clinic Philadelphia area-developer deal).

item19Available.note: Franchisor publishes Item 19 financial performance representations including quartile gross-sales data.

ownerTypes.note: Franchisor recruits both 'savvy entrepreneurs and chiropractors'.

brands.name: Crunch Fitness
brands.notes: Big-box HVLP (high-value, low-price) gym franchise with 500+ open locations, 3M+ members, and commitments for 1,300+ franchise locations. Capital-intensive (20,000–40,000 sq ft clubs) but one of the strongest Item 19s in fitness, ranked No. 20 overall in Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500.

riskFlags.title: Consumer class actions over membership billing practices
riskFlags.detail: An April 2026 California class action alleges Crunch uses 'drip pricing' — advertising membership prices while hiding a mandatory annual fee until late in checkout; earlier suits include a 2018 Pennsylvania auto-renewal class action and a TCPA collection-calls case. Brand-level consumer litigation, not franchisee-vs-franchisor disputes.

riskFlags.title: Capital-intensive model with heavy mandatory local ad spend
riskFlags.detail: Franchisor requires $500K liquid capital and $2.5M net worth, and mandates minimum local advertising of $15,000/month in year one (then the greater of $10,000/month or 7% of gross sales) on top of the 5% royalty and 2% brand fund.

nationwide.note: Franchisor states 'there are still great franchise territories available in all 50 states, as well as internationally' and that it sells well over 100 franchises per year (accessed June 2026).

liquidCapitalMin.note: $500,000 liquid capital plus $2.5M combined net worth; confirmed by Entrepreneur directory.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD range $928,000–$3,743,000. Conflicts: Entrepreneur's 2026 directory lists $2,147,500–$5,367,000 and the franchisor FAQ lists $668K–$3,488K excluding real estate — range varies widely by club format and real estate; FDD-derived 2025 figures used.

franchiseFee.note: Confirmed by Vetted Biz 2025 FDD summary.

adFundPercent.note: 2% of monthly gross sales to Brand Marketing Fund; separate mandatory local ad spend also applies (see riskFlags).

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19-derived: average yearly gross sales for clubs open an average of 37–48 months per Crunch's 2024 FDD Item 19; read Item 19 for defined terms and qualifiers — not all clubs included.

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 (2026); Vetted Biz lists 422 US franchised locations per 2025 FDD.

yearFounded.note: Brand founded 1989 in New York City; the franchising entity (Crunch Franchise) launched 2010, which Entrepreneur lists as its founding year.

minFootprint.note: Franchisor targets 20,000–40,000 sq ft with a 'sweet spot' of 25,000–30,000 sq ft and 125+ parking spots; lower bound shown.

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off franchise fee with purchase of 3+ clubs.

absenteeOwnership.note: Entrepreneur lists absentee ownership as allowed.

multiUnitAvailable.note: System is dominated by large multi-unit operators (e.g., Fitness Ventures); veteran incentive is tied to 3+ club commitments.

brands.name: F45 Training
brands.notes: Australian-founded 45-minute HIIT/functional group-training studio franchise, now part of FIT (Functional Inspired Training) house of brands with ~2,000 studios in 50+ countries. The brand suffered a severe post-IPO collapse (NYSE delisting, mass franchisee closures) and the US system is still contracting.

riskFlags.title: Post-IPO financial collapse and NYSE delisting
riskFlags.detail: F45 raised $325M+ at a ~$1B valuation in its July 2021 IPO, then missed new-opening estimates by ~60%; the stock fell from a $16 high to $0.53, CEO Adam Gilchrist resigned in July 2022, the company reported US$372.3M in losses, and it delisted from the NYSE and deregistered its stock in August 2023 — the incoming CEO called it a 'slow motion train wreck'.

riskFlags.title: Ongoing US system contraction and franchisee closures
riskFlags.detail: The US system lost 75 net locations in 2023 and another 38 net in 2024 (34 opened vs 72 exits: 46 terminations, 16 non-renewals, 10 ceased operations), ending 2024 at 753 US units; Entrepreneur's 2026 directory shows an -18% unit trend.

riskFlags.title: Shareholder securities suit settled for ~$11M
riskFlags.detail: A December 2022 shareholder lawsuit alleged F45 overstated growth potential in SEC filings tied to its IPO, naming board member Mark Wahlberg among directors; F45 agreed to pay nearly $11 million to settle.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Entrepreneur cash requirement $300,000; Vetted Biz lists a $90,000 minimum cash figure — conflict; Entrepreneur (more recent) used.

franchiseFee.note: Confirmed by Vetted Biz 2025 FDD summary; older sources cite $50K–$52K from prior FDD years.

royaltyPercent.note: 7% of monthly sales per 2025 FDD summary; F45 historically charged a flat monthly franchise fee before moving to percentage royalty.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD-derived yearly gross sales figure (2025 FDD, based on ~699 US studios). This is gross revenue, not earnings; other FDD-derived analyses report an average of $454,320 with median ~$407K. F45's Item 19 is revenue-only (no earnings representations).

totalUnits.note: Global unit count per Entrepreneur (declining -18%); US units were 753 at end of 2024 per FDD Item 20-derived data; franchisor site claims ~2,000 studios in 50+ countries.

closureCount.note: 2024 US exits per FDD Item 20-derived data: 46 terminations + 16 non-renewals + 10 ceased operations, against only 34 openings.

yearFounded.note: Franchisor states 'Founded in Australia in 2013'; Entrepreneur lists 2011 — franchisor's own statement used.

veteranDiscount.note: Up to 50% off the initial franchise fee for veterans.

exclusiveTerritories.note: FDD-derived summary: franchisees receive some territorial protection around the approved site but 'will not receive an exclusive territory'.

isGrowing.note: -18% unit trend per Entrepreneur; net US unit losses in both 2023 and 2024 per FDD Item 20-derived data.

item19Available.note: Item 19 discloses gross sales only — no earnings/profit representations.

value: pre-opening ad campaign build and management by franchisor
marketingSupport.note: Franchisor 'will help you build, run, and maintain ad campaigns' ahead of opening.

brands.name: Club Pilates
brands.notes: The largest Pilates studio franchise (1,300+ locations globally) and the flagship brand of Xponential Fitness (NYSE: XPOF). Strong unit economics and growth, but the parent company settled major FTC and franchisee actions in 2026 — diligence on Xponential disclosures is essential.

riskFlags.title: Parent Xponential Fitness FTC settlement — $17M back to franchisees
riskFlags.detail: The FTC secured a settlement (announced March 2026) against parent Xponential Fitness for Franchise Rule violations, with $17 million returned to franchisees — the largest consumer redress ever in an FTC franchise case. The FTC alleged Xponential misrepresented costs, risks, time to open/operate studios, and key company details across its brands, which include Club Pilates. Xponential agreed to pay over 12 months without admitting liability.

riskFlags.title: Parent settled with 500+ franchisees for $22.75M
riskFlags.detail: Alongside the FTC action, Xponential finalized a $22.75 million settlement with more than 500 of its franchisees (payable over 35 months), bringing total 2026 settlement obligations to nearly $40M; XPOF shares fell sharply on the news.

riskFlags.title: Parent-company turmoil: CEO ouster, SEC and DOJ scrutiny
riskFlags.detail: Xponential founder/CEO Anthony Geisler was forced out in May 2024 amid a federal probe; an SEC investigation opened in December 2023 (after the Fuzzy Panda short-seller report) closed in July 2025 without recommended action; a US Attorney (C.D. Cal.) investigation has also been reported.

nationwide.note: Franchisor FAQ lists available territories as nationwide (U.S.), with international master-franchise opportunities (accessed June 2026).

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $385,048–$839,058; matches Franchise Times Top 400 (2025) figures.

franchiseFee.note: Franchisor FAQ; reduced fees for additional units.

adFundPercent.note: 2% of gross sales to the Brand Development Fund.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19-derived: average gross revenue (AUV) per the 2025 FDD, based on 2024 data for the 849 studios reported in Item 19 (quartile data also disclosed).

totalUnits.note: Global units per Entrepreneur Franchise 500 (2026); ~1,029 US franchised units per 2025 FDD-derived data.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 4–8 employees; lower bound shown.

absenteeOwnership.note: Entrepreneur marks absentee ownership 'No', but franchisor FAQ supports a semi-absentee model where owners keep full-time jobs using a general manager and staff.

canRunPartTime.note: Entrepreneur lists part-time 'No'; franchisor FAQ says owners may keep full-time employment with a staffed studio (semi-absentee), which differs from running the studio part-time.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Reduced franchise fees for additional locations; single and multi-unit offerings.

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 includes average, median, high/low gross revenue by quartile for all reporting studios.

value: weekly sales and marketing calls pre-opening
ongoing weekly national sales calls and coaching

ownerTypes.note: FAQ: owners can maintain full-time jobs with a manager and staff, or operate full/part-time with adjusted staffing.

brands.name: Pure Barre
brands.notes: The largest barre fitness studio franchise (~620–650 studios across 48 US states plus Canada and Japan), founded 2001 and owned by Xponential Fitness since 2018. Unit count is roughly flat, and the Xponential parent-company FTC/franchisee settlements apply to diligence on this brand.

riskFlags.title: Parent Xponential Fitness FTC settlement — $17M back to franchisees
riskFlags.detail: The FTC secured a settlement (announced March 2026) against parent Xponential Fitness for Franchise Rule violations, with $17 million returned to franchisees — the largest consumer redress in an FTC franchise case. The FTC alleged misrepresentation of costs, risks, time to open and operate studios across Xponential's brands, which include Pure Barre.

riskFlags.title: Parent settled with 500+ franchisees for $22.75M
riskFlags.detail: Xponential finalized a $22.75 million settlement with more than 500 franchisees across its brands (payable over 35 months), in addition to the FTC settlement — nearly $40M in combined settlement obligations disclosed in 2026.

riskFlags.title: Flat-to-declining unit count and ~7% 3-year failure rate
riskFlags.detail: Pure Barre had 615 US franchised locations in 2024, but only 599 studios were franchisee-operated for the entire 2024 calendar year per the 2025 FDD; Vetted Biz calculates a 7% 3-year failure rate, and Entrepreneur shows unit growth of just +0.8%. Parent-company turmoil (CEO ouster May 2024; SEC probe closed July 2025 without action) also applies.

nationwide.source: Pure Barre official franchise page and available-markets portal
nationwide.note: Franchisor actively offers franchises US-wide (subject to franchise-registration-state compliance disclosed on the page) and maintains a dedicated territory-availability portal at franchise.purebarre.com/availablemarkets; brand already operates in 48 states (accessed June 2026).

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor FAQ: liquid assets greater than $250,000 and minimum net worth of $500,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $314,411–$629,345 per 2025 FDD Item 7; franchisor notes investment may be higher for New York City locations.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD-derived average annual studio revenue (AUV) per 2025 FDD analysis; Franchise Chatter's 2023 FDD analysis showed average sales of ~$306K — figure represents gross sales, not profit.

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 (2026); 615 US franchised locations in 2024 per FDD Item 20-derived data; franchisor site cites 650+ open studios including international.

absenteeOwnership.note: Entrepreneur lists absentee ownership not allowed; franchisor markets an 'executive model' (manager-run) which is semi-absentee, not absentee.

isGrowing.note: +0.8% unit growth per Entrepreneur — effectively flat; 2025 FDD Item 20 data shows fewer studios operated full-year 2024 than total 2024 count (see riskFlags).

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD contains Item 19 financial performance representations per Franchise Chatter's review.
